events.title: рҹҢҲ SIX the Musical - Opening Night
events.location: Steven Tanger Center for the Performing Arts
events.description:
ItвҖҷs time to take back the microphone and reclaim your crown!

We are SO excited to give away 4 tickets to the global sensation SIX for the opening night performance on Tuesday, May 5th, at the Tanger Center!

From Tudor Queens to Pop Icons, the SIX wives of Henry VIII remix five hundred years of historical heartbreak into a euphoric celebration of 21st-century power. The New York Times says SIX вҖңTOTALLY RULES!вҖқ and The Washington Post hails it as вҖңexactly the kind of energizing, inspirational illumination this town aches for!"

To enter: Tell us about a moment you felt fierce, stood up for yourself, or took back your narrative. We want to hear those "reclaim your crown" moments!

Two winners will be chosen to snag 2 tickets each for the May 5th show.

Show Details: Steven Tanger Center for the Performing Arts Tuesday, May 5th (Show runs May 5-10).

 If you can't make the opening night, you can get tickets from the link in the comments.

We canвҖҷt wait to read your stories below. LetвҖҷs show them how Greensboro shines!

#SIXTheMusical #GreensboroPride #IMPAQTGSO #TangerCenter #ReclaimYourCrown

events.title: рҹҺЁ Public Open Studio Hours
events.description:
MEET MACY вңЁ Current Artist in Residence, as part of the GPS Student Residency Program | Summer Studios: Arts on Site! вңЁ

About Macy macyjart 
Macy James (BFA Sculpture & Ceramics) will explore various materials that she has yet to try, though has long wanted to. Within this residency, Macy plans to work with more accessible material specifically, in addition to those she is accustomed to, like found and second-hand goods sourced from local places - materials that do not require large shops or expensive tools. Another material interest is found wood, like driftwood, to experiment with wood carving or wood burning alongside bead and wire sculptures, which she presently has no experience working with. The hope is that this will help her develop a healthy habit of creating art with widely accessible materials and make her an overall more multifaceted artist.

About the Student Residency Program:
Designed to support UNCG undergraduate student artists (BA + BFA) striving to develop, adapt, and/or reinvent their creative process and to promote artistic growth and development, artists are afforded two-weeks of uninterrupted research and development, coupled with financial support and public presentational platforms to share their creative work. 

Join our AIRs in their studio practices рҹ“…:
May 11-22, 2026

Public Open Studio Hours:
Monday, May 18th | 12-3P
Wednesday, May 20th | 2-5P

Closing Reception + Open Studio: 
Friday, May 22nd | 6-8P

Learn more by clicking вҖңHappening NowвҖқ in the Linktree above in our profile

events.title: рҹҺЁ Public Open Studio - Meet Elissa
events.description:
MEET ELISSA вңЁ Current Artist in Residence, as part of the GPS Student Residency Program | Summer Studios: Arts on Site! вңЁ

About Elissa elissa.artist 
Elissa Ukhueligbe (BFA Painting) plans to expand their practice through video and sound. The proposed idea is to animate photos into video and projections, mimicking a low вҖңframes per second.вҖқ Using film, photos, drawings, or smaller paintings, they would mix video/sound with traditional art. Elissa is a multi-media artist that uses portraits of women to communicate complex ideas about femininity, self-expression, and gendered norms. They are interested in psychological exploration; how the media we consume affects us internally and externally.

About the Student Residency Program:
Designed to support UNCG undergraduate student artists (BA + BFA) striving to develop, adapt, and/or reinvent their creative process and to promote artistic growth and development, artists are afforded two-weeks of uninterrupted research and development, coupled with financial support and public presentational platforms to share their creative work.

events.title: рҹҺЁ 4th Annual BA Student Exhibition
events.description:
A closer look into the 4th Annual BA Student Exhibition вңЁрҹҺЁ

Shoutout to our amazing BA student artists and the interns who helped curate this show.

*********************
NOW OPEN @ GPS
4th Annual BA Student Exhibition
Curated by GPS Interns:
Naomi Chavez, Kate Currivan, Melanie St. Clair
Exhibition: May 5-9, 2026
Reception: Thursday, May 7th, 6-8p
Gallery Open Hours:
Tuesday - Thursday 12-5p
Friday, CLOSED Saturday, 2-5p
This exhibition marks the fourth annual Bachelor of Arts student exhibition hosted by Greensboro Project Space. Students are given the opportunity to display the skills that they have built during their time as an undergraduate student at UNC Greensboro.
Greensboro Project Space is proud to showcase BA studentsвҖҷ ambition, drive, and creative power to our community. These students have formed their own journey on their own terms; exploring painting, drawing, printmaking, sculpture, animation, and new media. We invite you to experience and celebrate their creative development throughout their academic journey.
Learn more by clicking вҖңHappening NowвҖқ in the Linktree above in our profile

events.title: рҹҺЁ Homebody (MFA Thesis Exhibition by Kathryn Hood)
events.description:
Thank you to everyone who joined us for the reception of Homebody by Kathryn Hood!рҹҘӮ

The exhibition is still open until 5pm вҖ” donвҖҷt miss out on experiencing Homebody

************************
NOW OPEN @ GPS
Homebody

MFA Thesis Exhibition
by Kathryn Hood

Exhibition: April 28 - May 2, 2026
Reception: Friday, May 1st, 6-8p

Gallery Open Hours:
Tuesday - Friday 12-5p
Saturday, 2-5p

Homebody is an exhibition of collages and sculptures in which Kathryn Hood carves her perceptions of tense muscles into paper and upholstery foam. She embraces the body-mind connection, an essential tool for self-understanding, as she houses ungrounded avatars within handmade paper and muscular furnishings outside of formal traditions. Hood composes the interior feelings of ideas & emotions with the exterior feeling of tension. This duality of perception reflects the disparate nature of themes explored, such as femininity, domesticity, and the performance of identity.

Homebody is the residence of textured perceptions. This space is an act of resensitization.

Learn more by clicking вҖңHappening NowвҖқ in the Linktree above in our profile

events.title: рҹҺЁ Garden of Corporeal Fantasy
events.description:
Thank you to everyone who joined us in celebrating the reception Garden of Corporeal Fantasy by Victoria Mercado-Lues

The exhibition is still open until 5pm вҖ” step inside before it closes

************************
NOW OPEN @ GPS
Garden of Corporeal Fantasy
MFA Thesis Exhibition
by Victoria Mercado-Lues

Exhibition: April 21 - 25, 2026
Reception: Friday, April 24th, 6-8p

Gallery Open Hours:
Tuesday - Friday 12-5p
Saturday, 2-5p

Brightly saturated and fantastical, Garden of Corporeal Fantasy explores the meeting of nature and flesh in oil paintings and ceramics. With invented creatures and visceral biological imagery, the works reframe the gaze towards the bodily through a lens of wonder вҖ” a less punitive lens than the centuries-long and still recurring traditions of bodies as commodified, objectified, or sinful.

This exhibition addresses our discomfort surrounding the body by combining it with the godly imposing presence of the landscape, like waterfalls and billowing clouds. In this combination, the work is imbued with a devotional, almost sacred quality, borrowing visuals of the holy and filling it with the biological. The sticky, hairy, fleshy becomes something worth staring at with open fascination, the same way we are in awe of exotic plants, or deep-sea creatures, illuminating the sublime inherent in the corporeal.
Learn more by clicking вҖңHappening NowвҖқ in the Linktree above in our profile

events.title: рҹҺЁ Death Circle
events.description:
рқ”Үрқ”ўрқ”һрқ”ұрқ”Ҙ в„ӯрқ”Ұрқ”Ҝрқ” рқ”©рқ”ў, рқ”ұрқ”¬рқ”Ўрқ”һрқ”¶ рқ”Јрқ”Ҝрқ”¬рқ”Ә 5 рқ”ұрқ”¬ 7 рқ”“рқ”җ 

So many of us are carrying a lot right now. Personal losses, collective grief, the ache of what is happening in the world, and the grief we feel for our more-than-human kin and planet. We were never meant to carry any of this alone. What does it mean to live and die consciously? This circle is an offering to unburden ourselves in shared community and, in doing so, perhaps find more spaciousness and imaginative possibilities. 

Together we will co-create a sacred space to remember that these tender conversations about death, grief, cycles of change, and all of our connections in this web of existence once lived at the center of our communities. They are an integral part of our collective wellbeing. 

Death Circle is not a therapeutic setting or a grief support group, but an invitation to be present with one another as we explore these topics and reconnect more deeply with ourselves, each other, and the world around us. We welcome your curiosities, stories, questions, as well as your silence. There is no pressure to speak. All of it belongs рҹ–Ө

Our hope is that this offering fosters clarity, nurtures care, and deepens our relational intimacy. 

Death Circle will be held on the last Sunday of every month, from 5-7pm. WeвҖҷll have coffee and donuts, but feel free to bring your own snacks or any comfort items that support you.

We look forward to seeing you there вҖ” 
Joshua & Bri

events.title: рҹҺЁ Advance Base & Moontype with likewindowpanes
events.description:
advance_base & moontypo are coming back to Greensboro with local support from likewindowpanes on Wednesday, February 25th рҹ‘ҫрҹҺ¶рҹҺ№

Doors at 7pm and music at 8pm // $12 suggested 

This is going to be a really sweet show. WeвҖҷre super thrilled to have these folks back in Greensboro. If you missed Advance Base for his winter tour in 2024 or Moontype last summer, this is your chance to see them both with GreensboroвҖҷs own, Panes. 

Advance Base is the melancholic soft rock recording project of Chicago, IL singer/songwriter Owen Ashworth (formerly of Casiotone for the Painfully Alone). Using a two-handed arsenal of electric piano, Omnichord, samplers, effect pedals & drum machines, Ashworth builds minimalist, heavy-hearted, nostalgia-obsessed ballads around his conversational baritone. The warm, electronic sound of Advance Base has been described as вҖңlo-fi,вҖқ вҖңdepressedвҖқ & вҖңweirdly uplifting.вҖқ Advance Base released Horrible Occurrences in 2024.

Moontype is songwriter Margaret McCarthyвҖҷs dreamworld come to life. Based in Chicago, Illinois, the band features Emerson Hunton on drums, and Joe Suihkonen and Andrew Clinkman on guitar. Quiet emotions that McCarthy explores with her bass become, with the band, as large and as musically complex as a mood itself: cathartic, angsty, and unmet. Moontype released their debut album, Bodies of Water, in 2021 to critical acclaim.

panes is minimal introspective music with an engaging, dreamy listlessness. ever careening toward some kind of undiscovered self epiphany.

Flyer by: likewindowpanes

events.title: рҹҺЁ Cosmic Sound Exploration
events.description:
рқ—қрқ—јрқ—¶рқ—» рқҳӮрқҳҖ рқ—јрқ—» рқ—§рқ—өрқҳӮрқ—ҝрқҳҖрқ—ұрқ—®рқҳҶ, рқ—ҷрқ—Ірқ—Ҝрқ—ҝрқҳӮрқ—®рқ—ҝрқҳҶ рқҹӯрқҹөрқҳҒрқ—ө, рқ—ірқ—јрқ—ҝ рқ—® рқ—»рқ—¶рқ—ҙрқ—өрқҳҒ рқ—јрқ—і рқ—°рқ—јрқҳҖрқ—әрқ—¶рқ—° рқҳҖрқ—јрқҳӮрқ—»рқ—ұ рқ—Ірқҳ…рқ—Ҫрқ—№рқ—јрқ—ҝрқ—®рқҳҒрқ—¶рқ—јрқ—» рҹ”ҠрҹӘҗрҹҺөрҹҗҙ

рқҷҲрқҷ–рқҷҘрқҷЁ features Brian Haran (Ama Divers, Filthybirds; engineer on numerous records) handling electronics/oscillators/tapeloops, Nick Vanbuskirk on sax (Panes, Kill the Buddha, bedroom division, and countless others), along with Tyler Monroe behind the drums (plays with too many people to count). This project is 100% improvвҖ”noisy ambient colliding with avant jazz. brian.s.haran likewindowpanes _tyler.monroe_ 

рқҷҮрқҷӘрқҷ рқҷҡ рқҷҺрқҷҳрқҷқрқҷЈрқҷҡрқҷһрқҷҷрқҷҡрқҷ§ is a Nashville based pedal steel guitarist who has worked with a variety of artists including Margo Price, Orville Peck, S.G. Goodman, William Tyler, and Futurebirds. His debut solo album of ambient New Age music created entirely on pedal steel, вҖҳAltar of Harmony,вҖҷ was released by Third Man Records in 2020. His new EP вҖҳFor Dancing In Quiet LightвҖҷ is out now via Leaving Records. lukefromnashville 

рқҷҺрқҷ–рқҷ–рқҷҘрқҷ–рқҷ©рқҷӨ is the music project of upstate New YorkвҖ“based sound artist Brendan Principato, who weaves manipulated field recordings with lush electronic soundscapes exploring ecology and music. He has held residencies with the National and Alaska State Park Services, including 2023 near Juneau and 2022 on Fire Island. His recordings have been named Best Ambient on Bandcamp, and his live performances include collaborations with Laraaji, Don Slepian, Patricia Wolf, and Green-House. saapato

events.title: рҹҺЁ DAGGERTEETH
events.description:
DAGGERTEETH TOMORROW TOMORROW TOMORROW.
.
ARE Y'ALL PUMPED CUZ WE CAN'T WAIT TO OPEN OUR DOORS TO YOU TOMORROW!
.
Teeth don't lie. They bite, they tear, they mark what they touch.

Traditional printmaking techniques meet zine culture's raw urgency. Punk aesthetics slash through the white cube aesthetic and screams to be heard.

Every piece here carries evidence of handsвҖ”ink rolled, paper pressed, screens burned, images carved. The tactility of making things physical in a digital age. These works don't apologize for their rough edges. They weaponize them.

DAGGERTEETH celebrates artists who create outside institutional approval, distribution systems built on photocopiers and kitchen tables, aesthetics born from necessity and defiance. The underground made visible.

Sharp work for sharp teeth.

**February 21, 2026 | 5PM-8PM**

Irving Park Art & Frame

2800 Lawndale Dr. Ste 107, Greensboro, NC

events.title: рҹҺЁ LEAF LITTER: Abalone Variation 1вҖ“6 by KT Wilson
events.description:
WeвҖҷre thrilled to spotlight a truly captivating series in our current exhibition, LEAF LITTERвҖ”вҖҳAbalone Variation 1вҖ“6вҖҷ by the talented artist KT Wilson. These original works are a celebration of the subtle, often overlooked wonders of nature, and we invite you to experience them in person at Irving Park Art & Frame.
Each piece in this series features a sustainably sourced abalone shell (pДҒua) from the pristine waters of New Zealand. Revered for their iridescent beauty and cultural significance, New Zealand abalone shells are harvested under one of the worldвҖҷs most robust sustainability systems, ensuring that their natural populations are protected for generations to come. These shells, considered вҖҳtaongaвҖҷ (treasures) in MДҒori culture, are not only a feast for the eyes but also a testament to the harmony of art and ecological responsibilityвҒ .
KT WilsonвҖҷs approach transforms each shell into a meditative focal point by mounting them in custom shadowbox frames. Rather than traditional fastenings, Wilson uses carefully woven textiles and thread to cradle each shell in place. This technique both secures the shell and highlights its natural form, color, and texture, drawing attention to the unique qualities of each specimen. The interplay between the organic shell and the geometric textile work invites viewers to contemplate the contrast and connection between the natural world and human creativity.
The вҖҳAbalone VariationвҖҷ series is a perfect fit for LEAF LITTER, our exhibition dedicated to the nuanced, often unnoticed aspects of nature. This show brings together artists whose work delves into the delicate, the intricate, and the ephemeralвҖ”reminding us that beauty is often found in quiet details. KT WilsonвҖҷs pieces exemplify this ethos, offering viewers a chance to slow down and appreciate the subtle complexity of natural materials and thoughtful craft.
.
Visit us at Irving Park Art & Frame to see KT WilsonвҖҷs work and explore the full LEAF LITTER exhibition. Support local art, sustainable materials, and the ongoing conversation between human hands and the natural world.
.
.
.
#ArtExhibition #ContemporaryArt #NatureInspired #KTWilson #AbaloneArt #SustainableArt #PauaShel

Their debut record CryinвҖҷ on the Vine, features вҖңStrange WorldвҖҷ...which won best R&B song of 2010 from the international John Lennon Songwriting Contest. Their second release Elephant Graveyard features the talents of Kofi Burbridge (Tedeschi/Trucks Band) and showcases the groupвҖҷs vast musical range, capturing moments of spinster storytelling to raw and unfettered revelry

events.title: рҹҺө Live Music Performance
events.description:
Tomorrow, Sunday the 7th!
Doors at 6:30pm
Show at 7:30pm

Mason P. Winfree is an award-winning old-time musician and flatfoot dancer who has earned numerous ribbons, including first-place honors, at bluegrass and old-time fiddlersвҖҷ conventions across North Carolina and Southwest Virginia, such as the Galax Old Fiddlers Convention and the Mount Airy Fiddlers Convention. Over the years, he has performed with several groups and has shared his craft by teaching and showcasing flatfoot dancing both nationally and abroad, including on PBSвҖҷs Song of the Mountains. *

Folksinger Jodi Jones is determined to boldly dream a better tomorrow in the wake of a broken and weary world. Deeply rooted in the musical traditions of her childhood home in the hills of Western Pennsylvania, her work is a poetic and unflinching account of the beauty and hardship of working class life in America. With defiant wit and innate musicality, she beautifies the bitter truths of our modern age into song as an act of love and solidarity. Art may not save us from our problems, but Jones believes it is crucial to surviving them with our humanity intact.
To sing is to live, to live is to hope, and to hope is to find the strength to fight another day in these dark and uncertain times. With guitar, banjo, fiddle, and even dulcimer in hand, she seeks to alchemize pain into purpose and break old ground to plant new seeds of hope and change wherever she goes.
Jack Romano & The Romanaires create a mythic sound of the 20th century as a backdrop for stories from modern American life.

Jack was born in the New Jersey highlands, a unique cultural region whose вҖңhill peopleвҖқ share as much in common with Appalachian communities in West Virginia as with cities like New York City and Philadelphia. He grew up listening to artists like Johnny Cash, John Denver, and Gene Autry, alongside Frank Sinatra, Dion & the Belmonts, and The Spinners.

events.title: рҹҺө Megan JeanвҖҷs Secret Family
events.description:
Tonight, Thursday the 4th!
Doors at 7pm
Show at 8pm

Megan JeanвҖҷs Secret Family is a fresh blend of the 70вҖҷs by way of the 90вҖҷs rock, soul, country, and jam, emphasizing vocal-driven danceable original songs that bring together generations of sound. In May of 2024, the band made their national radio debut on NPRвҖҷs Mountain Stage alongside notable acts like Keller Williams and Bettye Lavette. DC Music Review called them one of their вҖңNew favorite actsвҖқ following an energitc performance at 2024вҖҷs DelFest. The band was awarded a creativity grant from the Maryland State Arts Council to produce their debut full-length album PLOT (Play.Loud.On.Turntable) which will be released in Fall 2024. In the three years since their formation they have shared the stage with Fastball, The Spin Doctors, Cabinet, Joan Osbourne, and Cris Jacobs.

events.title: рҹҺө Tom Hamilton - Live Performance
events.description:
Tomorrow, Friday the 5th!
Doors at 8pm
Show at 9pm

Tom HamiltonвҖҷs guiding creative principle is very simple: he doesnвҖҷt like to repeat himself artistically.For the Philadelphia-based singer/songwriter andguitarist, this mindset stems from a deep-seated motivation to keep pushing himself as an artist вҖ”to delve into different lyrical themes and musical detours, to explore potentially uncomfortable and unfamiliar emotional places.вҖқThatвҖҷs a thrilling prospect,вҖқ says Hamilton. вҖңTo find the deeper things inside and to open up those doors that I didnвҖҷt even know were there.вҖқAs founding member of Brothers Past, American Babies, Joe RussoвҖҷs Almost Dead, Ghost Light and Billy and The Kids, Hamiltonhas written/recorded roughlya dozen albums and EPs and shared the stage with everyone from John Mayer to Bob Weir to Billy Strings.HamiltonвҖҷs debut record as a solo artist will be released in late 2025.

events.title: рҹҺө McKinney & MAMA
events.description:
Tonight, Sunday the 31st!
Doors at 7:30pm
Show at 8pm

McKinney is a bass player and singer-songwriter. She spent her formative years in southern West Virginia where she literally LIVED inside a music school. Deeply incapable of leaving the Appalachian mountains, but needing an escape from her 6,000 person hometown, she landed right around the bend in Asheville, NC.В 

When McKinney isnвҖҷt on stage you might find her teaching music lessons at LEAF Global Arts, working shows at The Orange Peel, or at lock on the rugby pitch.В 

McKinneyвҖҷs music genre is about as fluid as the average Asheville residentвҖҷs sexuality. Her Indie Pop sound is blended with influence from her blues singer mother, a BFA in Jazz, and the 8 year old version of herself that just wants to play classic rock. Although pinning down her genre may be hard, you can consistently expect sad lyrics. You will probably enjoy her lyricism if you are emotionally suppressed and enjoy listening to sad songs to make yourself feel better.В В 

MAMA is a Soul ShakinвҖҷ Blues project from Asheville, NC. Fronted by Melissa McKinney, a vocalist known for her raw power, emotional delivery, and genuine connection with her audience, the band is quickly making a name for themselves in the blues world. MAMA earned Second Place at the International Blues Challenge this year in Memphis, TN. They also won the Merlefest Band Competition in 2025. Beyond their accolades, Melissa is a leader in the Asheville arts community. She serves as Programming Director for LEAF Global Arts, is the co-founder of the WTF (Women to the Front) Music Festival, and a board member of AVL Fest.

events.title: рҹҺө Tommy Prine with Brad Goodall
events.description:
Tonight, Saturday the 30th!
Doors at 7pm
Show at 8pm

Tommy Prine is a Nashville-bred songwriter carving out his own identity with unflinching honesty, dynamic guitar work, and a voice that bridges Americana, indie folk, and Southern storytelling. Following the breakout of his acclaimed debut albumВ This Far SouthВ and the momentum around his new EPВ Love CircleВ (out now), Prine has emerged as one of the most compelling young artists in the scene вҖ” known for emotionally charged live shows, sharp songwriting, and a rapidly growing fanbase across the U.S. and Europe.

West Virginia songwriter and piano bard Brad Goodall established himself as a vivid storyteller with his 2022 debut Made In America, which boasted uproariously satirical character studies of sad, desperate men in the vein of Randy Newman and Warren Zevon. Where that album unfolded like a collection of biting short stories, his latest Hometown plays out like an autobiography..

events.title: рҹҺө Kevn Kinney Live
events.description:
Tonight, Thursday the 28th!
Doors at 7pm
Show at 8pm

Kevn Kinney is most widely known as founder and frontman of Atlanta-based rock band DrivinN-Cryin. A highly-acclaimed singer, songwriter, musician, performer, visual artist and poet, heВ 
has recently been inducted into both the Wisconsin Music Hall of Fame and Georgia Music HallВ 
of Fame for his impactful legacy of art.
Kinney has released multiple critically acclaimed solo and band recordings during his careerВ 
having also collaborated with Warren Haynes, REM, The Who and John Popper, among others.В 
Peter Buck of REM has produced and performed on several of KevnвҖҷs albums and continues toВ 
be involved in his career.
Kevn Kinney has continuously toured full-time with his band Drivin n Cryin while simultaneouslyВ 
carving out a niche for himself as a solo artist. From the festival stage to the smallest houseВ 
concert, fans are always excited at the opportunity to see him up close and personal in aВ 
stripped-down acoustic setting.В 
Recent touring include multiple dates with the Indigo Girls, Blackberry Smoke, The BlackВ 
Crowes and others.
His latest solo studio album Think About It was released in 2022. In Fall 2023 the first release ofВ 
the LetвҖҷs Go DancinвҖҷ: A Compilation Celebrating The Songs Of Kevn Kinney four-album vinylВ 
series came out with over 100 digital recorded titles slated to go through end of 2024. TheВ 
project is a massive all-star tribute to songwriter, poet, performer Kevn Kinney. Tracks fromВ 
Jason Isbell, Violent Femmes, Shovels & Rope, Mike Farris, Great Peacock and dozens moreВ 
will be released.

events.title: рҹӨқ Wild World of Animal Survival
events.description:
Get ready to dive into the wild world of animal survival! Through hands-on science fun with microscopes, real specimens, stories, and games, children will uncover the fascinating ways creatures protect, hunt, and thrive. Scientist and author, Dawn Cusick, will share from her adventurous books "Animals That Make Me Say Ouch!" and "Animals That Make Me Say Look Out!".

This event introduces amazing animals вҖ” from alligators and snakes to jellyfish, bats, and beetles through microscopes, games and specimens. Discover how each one uses its unique adaptations to stay one step ahead in natureвҖҷs game!

events.title: рҹӨқ The Intense Audiovisuals of Hatsune Miku
events.description:
Greensboro Public Library Anime Con is delighted to announce our next featured panelist!

Dr. Clair Nguyen serves as Lecturer in Music Theory at UNC Greensboro. Her primary research focuses on the intersections of music theory and film & media studies that includes films, music videos, video games, theme park music, Japanese anime, and Hatsune Miku rhythm games. Her secondary research area centers on Vietnamese popular and traditional music, including the vб»Қng cб»• tradition.  She currently serves as advisor for the UNCG Animation Club as well as co-chair for the Society of Music TheoryвҖҷs Film and Multimedia interest group.

She will be presenting her panel "The Intense Audiovisuals of Hatsune Miku," an exploration of how synchronized sounds and onscreen action create thoughtful and powerful storytelling in Vocaloid and Hatsune Miku: Colorful Stage! music videos.

Come meet Clair and join in on the fun on Saturday, August 8 from 10 am to 5 pm!

events.title: рҹӨқ Dreams - AAPI Heritage Month Cinema Screening
events.description:
Our AAPI Heritage Month Cinema series continues with a screening of "Dreams", an anthology film directed by acclaimed Japanese filmmaker Akira Kurosawa.

Eight visually rich vignettes drawn from KurosawaвҖҷs own dreamsвҖ”fox weddings and vanished orchards, a soldierвҖҷs ghosts, a walk through Van GoghвҖҷs canvases, nuclear nightmares, and a water-mill utopiaвҖ”meditate on childhood, art, mortality, and humanityвҖҷs uneasy bond with nature.

The film is rated PG.

events.description:
Merry Christmas & Happy Holidays рҹҺ„ As we approach the end of the year , we are thankful for all of the love and support weвҖҷve gotten thus far. ItвҖҷs never easy starting something new, youвҖҷve gotta just put your best foot forward and go. events.title: рҹӨқ Arrival of Cele Fierro in Buenos Aires
events.description:
"On the morning of Tuesday, October 7, the Argentine Embassy in Tel Aviv reported that Israeli authorities would transfer our comrade Cele Fierro along with other activists from Ketziot prison to the Jordanian border for release.

Our comrade Cele, who was aboard the Adara, along with two other Argentines, will fly out of Jordan early in the morning of Wednesday, October 8 (local time), to arrive in Buenos Aires later that same night.

We are proud of CeleвҖҷs internationalist militancy, putting her body on the line in the risky mission of the GSF, representing the MST of Argentina and the International Socialist League (ISL)." ligainternacionalsocialista cele_fierro

Dozens chanted long live the Intifada and even 1,2,3 Fuck the bourgeoisie! It was great seeing everyone, solidarity forever!! Stay tuned for a post regarding the next coalition public forum (from IDF to ICE) on October 3!
 Group of revolutionary socialists in Greensboro, NC and members of Tempest Collective @tempest_mag

events.title: рҹӨқ Greensboro Revolutionary Socialists Special Meeting: Borders are prisons and prisons are borders
events.description:
Greensboro Revolutionary Socialists would like to invite you to join us this Monday, February 10 at 7pm at 1333 Grove St for a special meeting, featuring a talk by organizer Kristen Godfrey on their article, "Borders are prisons and prisons are borders" with a discussion to follow.

*

"The way working-class people experience borders all over the world is tied to the theft of land and surplus value. Settler-colonialismвҖҷs life source is criminality and illegality, which is why we must be anti-capitalist abolitionists, developing a critical analysis of how imperialistsвҖҷ tactics work to divide us."

*

Tell us how this new context impacts you. Let's think about how we can resist together.
 Group of revolutionary socialists in Greensboro, NC and members of Tempest Collective @tempest_mag

events.title: рҹҺө WAXX OFF Debut at Elsewhere
events.description:
Welcoming the debut to elsewhere of AustraliaвҖҷs homegrown UKG star waxxoffmusic this July. Tickets on sale in bio now

WAXX OFF is a frontline dj and producer from adelaide, south australia, shaping australiaвҖҷs current wave of underground uk garage and speed garage. renowned for high-impact, high energy performances, his sets command the dance-floor through purposeful, momentum-driven programming. his productions have gained global traction through releases on influential labels including ec2a, sweat it out and no instruction, earning support from key figures such as interplanetary criminal, malugi, marlon hoffstadt and dr dubplate just to name just a few.following a year of relentless touring throughout australia, new zealand and asia in 2025; including standout performances at a3 festival, beyond the valley and wildlands, waxx off has firmly established himself as one of the countryвҖҷs most exciting producers and selectors.beyond his solo output, his work with ukg outfit inside kru has delivered beatport top 100 charting records and multiple national sellouts, further solidifying his position within the scene entering 2026 with a wave of new music and clear momentum, waxx off is positioned for his biggest year to date

events.title: рҹҺө Spotlight Launch Party - Latour b2b Jimmy D
events.description:
This Saturday we host the launch party of the new joint record from elsewhereвҖҷs two music directors Latour + Jimmy D, whoвҖҷve teamed up to release вҖңSpotlightвҖқ on the iconic Central Station Records. 

Latour b2b Jimmy D for a 3.5hr set this Saturday
with support from Rav & Cobella. 

About the record:
Latour and Jimmy D are two music directors on the Gold Coast at elsewhere whoвҖҷve come together to write Spotlight. (their second collab)

JimmyвҖҷs history dates back to being a vinyl DJ on the Sydney circuit in the 90вҖҷs, we wanted to bring back the flavour of that era with new updated NRG, that holds weight on todayвҖҷs systems and events. 

The record being a standout memorable tune of the night with its stellar vocal which sees the majority of the record stripped away leaving a sassy ear catching moment in the rave. Then slowly creeping back up to an all our NRG finale. Think donk basslines, 90s rave synths & fast grooving raw drums.

A record to make SURE the dancefloor is indeed dancing. And if you arenвҖҷt then, вҖңgetttt the f offfffff вҖң

events.title: рҹӨқ TowneBank Summer Concert Series: The Shakedown
events.description:
Yesterday, we had the pleasure of celebrating the talented student artists behind this yearвҖҷs Downtown Signal Box Student Art Project.

Surrounded by family, friends, and supporters, these young creatives were recognized for transforming ordinary signal boxes into vibrant works of public art that add color, creativity, and personality to downtown Greensboro.

Students received goodie bags and special maps highlighting the locations of signal boxes featuring student artwork, giving them the chance to explore downtown and see how their creativity is making a lasting impact on our community.

The celebration will continue this Thursday when artwork from several student participants is featured on the video board during the TowneBank Summer Concert Series at First National Bank Field as The Shakedown takes the stage.

To every student who shared their creativity with our community: thank you. Your artwork helps make downtown Greensboro a more vibrant, welcoming, and inspiring place for everyone. To the teachers who made this project possible, thank you for investing in your students and encouraging them to participate! 

Join us in congratulating these incredible young artists and enjoy a look back at yesterdayвҖҷs reception through these photos.

events.title: рҹӨқ Surprise Appreciation Event for Downtown Ambassadors
events.description:
Our friends at irc__greensboro approached our team about throwing a surprise appreciation event for our Downtown Ambassadors! 

We are always happy to show our gratitude to the Crown Jewels of Downtown Greensboro! 

Business owners, former Ambassadors, and some of our city partners joined us in the surprise and even shared some kind words. 

Thank you so much to Deonna from the IRC, Alexis from chandlersdtgreensboro, Angelina & Jake from find_your_flow_ , Andy from glasbear_ , thequartergso, our business owners community, and our friends at cityofgreensboro  gsoparksandrec for celebrating our Ambassadors today! 

Next time youвҖҷre downtown and see one of our friends, give them a wave or say hello! 

WE LOVE YOU! рҹ’ҡ

The bodyarmorstategames are bringing PROFESSIONAL sumo wrestlers to Downtown Greensboro for First Saturday Stroll on Elm!

June 5th:

events.title: рҹҢҲ Pride Celebration at Pyrle Theater
events.description:
Are you ready to celebrate Pride?!? Pride in our gender identity, gender expression, and sexual orientation happens year-round, and it's also great to have opportunities to celebrate together!

Join us at the Pyrle Theater in downtown Greensboro for a day of celebration on Saturday, June 20. The fun kicks off in the afternoon with family-friendly songs led by Mr. Sam of Scuppernong's Saturday Get-Downs, followed by a pizza dance party for youth and teens. To close out the night, the 21+ crowd will be treated to performances by drag superstars Thigh Fieri, Franny Starlight, and Brenda the Drag Queen, with DJ SmoothE providing tunes throughout the evening.

The Pyrle will have food and drinks available to keep the fun going. Tickets will be available soon through the Pyrle, so stay tuned!

events.title: рҹҺӯ Harry Connick Jr. at DPAC
events.description:
рҹҺ¶ From Carnegie Hall to DPAC рҹҺ¶
Harry Connick Jr.вҖҷs sold-out performance at Carnegie Hall was the fulfillment of a lifelong dream he shared with his mother, who always hoped to see him play there before she passed away from ovarian cancer. Forty-five years later, on what would have been her 100th birthday, he honored her wish on that same stage. 
We can't wait to welcome him to DPAC as he continues his inspirational journey by bringing his music and stories to our stage for one night only, July 5.
рҹҺҹпёҸ at DPACnc.com
DPAC presented by First Horizon

events.title: рҹӨқ Lambs & Clams Reunion at the Finale
events.description:
It's been incredible to be back in Charleston for the 20th anniversary of chswineandfood, and to share this time with echolocate (my oldest kiddo). 

Tonight's our main event: the Lambs & Clams reunion at the Finale! 

But first these two introverted extroverts need to find a quiet beach to recharge our batteries.

events.title: рҹҺЁ Summer Wars Screening
events.description:
When shy virtual reality moderator Kenji agrees to pose as the pretty and popular Natsuki's affluent fake boyfriend, a rogue AI steals his identity and hacks the virtual reality system. Accused of causing real-world catastrophes, he must unite Natsuki's family to stop an impending cyber apocalypse.

On Saturday, June 13, we are not only hosting our biggest NCMA Loves Anime Bazaar yet, but afterward, the Triangle Choir Club will perform anime songs, followed by an 8 pm screening of Mamoru Hosoda's "Summer Wars" (summary above).

Both of these events are included with your ticket for the film screening, so make sure to stick around for more anime fun!

Get your ticket at the link in our bio.

---------
Image: Courtesy of GKIDS

events.title: рҹҺЁ Screening of 'Always Looking: Titus Brooks Heagins'
events.description:
Reminder: Join us on Friday evening, June 5, for a screening of the 2025 documentary, "Always Looking: Titus Brooks Heagins."

This film looks at the work of photographer Titus Brooks Heagins, whose photos document those marginalized by race, gender/sexual identity, and poverty. The film also explores his own challenges of capturing these stories while posing the question, "who can tell whose story?"

Get your tickets at the link in our bio.

events.title: рҹҺЁ Still. Moving free dance performance
events.description:
The next "Still. Moving" free dance performance at the NCMA will be this Saturday, June 6, at 11 am and 1 pm in the Museum Park. Join us as Frankie Lee III (fleedance) presents a new work inspired by Wim Botha's sculptures, "Prism 5" and "Prism 7."

BothaвҖҷs sculptures explore weighty questions of history, status, power, and religion. Suspended wings hover above the ground, liftedвҖ”and restrainedвҖ”by thin rectangular supports that seem to both elevate and tether them.

Are these the wings of unseen, silent angels? Or do they belong to something darker? Through movement, Lee and the dancers enter that tension between ascension and gravity, freedom and control.

--------
Images 1 and 3: Wim Botha, Prism 5 (2013) and Prism 7 (2014), bronze and stainless steel, dimensions variable, Purchased with funds from the Modern and Contemporary Deaccession Fund
Image 2: Frankie Lee III, courtesy of the artist

events.title: рҹҺЁ NCMA Dance Gumbo
events.description:
Sure, it's Monday, but let's look ahead to this Saturday - if you want to get your weekend started with a good workout, come on out to the Museum Park at 9 am on June 6 for NCMA Dance Gumbo!

Led by instructor Jordan Farrell, this class blends pop, Zumba, Latin, hip-hop, and more into an energetic, all-levels aerobic workout that celebrates movement, music, and culture.

Get your free tickets at the link in our bio.

----------
Photo: Courtesy of the instructor

events.title: рҹҺЁ Please Be Seated - North Carolina: Dance Activation
events.description:
The fencing around the Museum Park's latest installation, "Please Be Seated - North Carolina," created by PaulCocksedge, is coming down! We'll be celebrating the official public opening tomorrow, Tuesday, June 2, with a special dance activation.

Directed by choreographers Christa Oliver and Betsy Graves, the performance features live musicians alongside over 40 dancers from ThePanoramicDanceProject (NCSU), Broughton Dance (CapsDance), and communities across North Carolina.

The performance begins at 6:30 pm and is free to attend. The dance should last about 15 minutes, so make sure to get there on time!

Click the link in our bio for more details.

events.title: рҹҺЁ Please Be Seated - North Carolina Opening Dance Performance
events.description:
Exciting news! The NCMA's latest outdoor installation, "Please Be Seated - North Carolina," created by PaulCocksedge, will open to the public in just a few days - on Tuesday, June 2.

To celebrate, we are hosting a special dance performance at 6:30 pm sharp featuring live musicians and dozens of dancers from ThePanoramicDanceProject, Broughton Dance (CapsDance), and communities across the state.

Join us for a vibrant celebration of movement, music, and public art!

Click the link in our bio for all the details.

events.title: рҹҺЁ Still. Moving: Zonal Harmonic 170,000 and Zonal Harmonic 220,000
events.description:
Interpreted by Rainbow Dance Company Director of Dance Kathryn Auman, this Saturday's free "Still. Moving: Zonal Harmonic 170,000 and Zonal Harmonic 220,000" performance will explore the unseen architectures that shape how we move, gather, and belong.

Inspired by TomГЎs SaracenoвҖҷs vision of airborne networks and interspecies connection, Rainbow Dance/ArtsTogether will perform a piece that considers the body not as an isolated figure but as part of a larger, vibrating systemвҖ”held in tension, resonance, and relation.

A brief talkback with Auman and the dancers follows the performance.

Join us in West Building at either 11 am or 1 pm on May 30 to see art in motion - and watch the video to see clips of Rainbow Dance/Arts Together's previous Still. Moving interpretation of Yoan Capote's "Isla (Tierra Prometida)."

events.title: рҹҺЁ NCMA Loves Anime
events.description:
Our celebration of everything anime, NCMA Loves Anime, returns on Saturday, June 13, and this summer's event is going to be bigger than ever!

NCMA Loves Anime will feature anime vendors, music, and food, followed by a ticketed evening screening of Mamoru Hosoda's 2009 film "Summer Wars."

We will have both new vendors, such as PlantformerCreations, MsBobaCaps, and CaitlynKurilich, and returning favorites, like Boxuchan, ChunkieBunnieArt, and plenty more. The CrispyGyoza food truck will be on site from 11:30 am to 2 pm and from 5 to 8 pm.

Check out the video for a quick tour of NCMA Loves Anime's winter bazaar.

Note: Cosplayers are welcome, but please leave the weapons/props at home and keep in mind that this is an all-ages, family-friendly event!

-------
Note: This post has been updated to reflect that the film screening is a ticketed event.

events.title: рҹҺЁ From Irving Berlin to Louis Armstrong
events.description:
Tickets are running low for Thursday evening's concert, "From Irving Berlin to Louis Armstrong," featuring renowned internationally touring jazz pianist JoeAlterman and his quartet, plus special guest ChristianTamburrProductions!

This concert celebrates the extraordinary creative exchange between Jewish composers and African American performers that helped define the sound of the 20th century. Join us at 7 pm on May 28 as these musicians explore iconic works by legendary figures such as Irving Berlin, George Gershwin, Duke Ellington, and Louis Armstrong.

Get your tickets at the link in our bio before they sell out!

events.title: рҹҺЁ Still. Moving dance performance (11am)
events.description:
Our next "Still. Moving" dance performance will be on Saturday, May 30, at 11 am and 1 pm in West Building. Created in dialogue with TomГЎs SaracenoвҖҷs "Zonal Harmonic 170,000" and "Zonal Harmonic 220,000," this original work by Rainbow Dance Co./Arts Together explores the unseen architectures that shape how we move, gather, and belong.

Inspired by SaracenoвҖҷs vision of airborne networks and interspecies connection, the choreography considers the body not as an isolated figure but as part of a larger, vibrating systemвҖ”held in tension, resonance, and relation. Like SaracenoвҖҷs sculptural environments, the dance piece asks: What holds us up, and what holds us together?

Both performances are free to attend, and no registration is required. You can read more about this performance at the link in our bio.

--------
Image 1: TomГЎs Saraceno, Zonal Harmonic 170,000, 2022, carbon fiber, polyester rope, monofilament, H. 34 5/8 Г— W. 56 5/16 Г— D. 46 7/16 in., Gift of Richard DeWid and Helen Murray with additional funds from the North Carolina State Art Society (Robert F. Phifer Bequest) 

TomГЎs Saraceno, Zonal Harmonic 220,000, 2022, carbon fiber, polyester rope, monofilament, H. 73 5/8 Г— W. 103 15/16 Г— D. 102 3/8 in., Gift of Richard DeWid and Helen Murray with additional funds from the North Carolina State Art Society (Robert F. Phifer Bequest) 

Image 2: Rainbow Dance Co.: Courtesy of the company

events.title: рҹҺЁ Knowing the West Community Day
events.description:
Looking for something to do today, but don't feel like being out in the rain? You're in luck! 

Today is our Knowing the West Community Day, which means you get free entry into our new exhibition, Knowing the West. Additionally, from 11 am-4 pm, we will be hosting the Western North Carolina Art and Craft Showcase, a free one-day market celebrating the traditions and contemporary practices of craft artists from WNC, alongside makers from across the state.

Visit us today to get a new perspective on the American West, then meet North Carolina artists, explore handmade work, and experience what makes the NCMA a statewide gathering place for creativity, connection, and cultural celebration!

Entry to both events is free, and no registration is required.

-----------
Pictured: Olive Rush, Maria and Julian, 1914, oil on academy board, 12 1/4 Г— 18 1/2 in., Tia Collection, Santa Fe, NM; Image: James Hart Photography

events.title: рҹҺЁ Hildaland
events.description:
This weekend! DonвҖҷt miss two incredible shows at The ArtsCenter. 

Firstly, on Thursday night donвҖҷt miss the chance to see New England duo Hildaland as they continue their tour through the southeast! 

A contemporary collage of Scottish and American traditional music, Hildaland is a duo that approaches geographic borders as markers of exploration rather than boundaries to be wary of. Fiddle player Louise Bichan, originally from Orkney, Scotland, and mandolinist Ethan Setiawan, originally from Goshen, Indiana, have taken their duo from traditional Scottish roots through to progressive bluegrass and jazz, with music inspired largely by places and the journeys between them, both physical and musical. 

Then on Saturday, Pop Up Chorus returns with a special Broadway edition centering the songs from the smash hit musical Hamilton, which is celebrating its 11th year with a national tour now playing at DPAC! Come sing songs from the show with PopUp Broadway here at The ArtsCenter on June 6!

Tickets on sale at artscenterlive.org

events.title: рҹҺЁ Horse Lords
events.description:
JUST ANNOUNCED: SUNDAY 10/11 8:00 PM вҖ” Horse Lords (local support TBD) 

Horse Lords were founded in Baltimore, Maryland in 2010; they evolved from the experimental collective Teeth Mountain and began as a trio with drummer Sam Haberman, guitarist Owen Gardner, and bassist Max Eilbacher, soon adding alto saxophonist Andrew Bernstein to the core ensemble. Though Horse Lords grew out of the fertile Baltimore noise and leftfield rock scene, a storied environment for artists and weirdos that has nurtured many an influential outsider band (Lungfish, Matmos), their approach is more omnivorous than the stippled rhythms of instrumental electric rock would indicate. 

Across sixteen years of frequent touring, Horse Lords have released ten studio, live, and collaboration albums in addition to a split EP with fellow Baltimoreans Lower Dens, a handful of singles and compilation tracks. For RVNGвҖҷs FRKWYS series, the band collaborated with Berlin-based composer Arnold Dreyblatt, a onetime student of La Monte Young and Pauline Oliveros whose just intonation chamber music was a crucial part of the post-Fluxus Downtown New York City environment. 

With the relocation of three of the four members to Germany beginning in 2021, the potential for more further creative exploration, and performance opportunities including the Moers Festival (with an 11-piece lineup, interpreting Julius EastmanвҖҷs music as well as their own), the interdisciplinary phases of the bandвҖҷs music was poised for fascinating new directions. The band observes вҖңWe are always trying to balance working within the limitations of the quartet and pushing beyond them.

For their second studio album on RVNG Intl., Demand To Be Taken To Heaven Alive!, Horse Lords have followed this notion and augmented the instrumentation, expanding the format slightly to include bass clarinetist Madison Greenstone (TAK Ensemble), trombonist Weston Olencki (Nate Wooley, RAGE Thormbones) and vocalists Nina Guo (Departure Duo) and Evelyn Saylor (Holly Herndon, Caterina Barbieri) across a program of twelve original compositions. 

Read more/buy tickets at https://artscenterlive.org/performances-events

events.title: рҹҺЁ Friday Night Vision Boarding with an Eye on the Summer
events.description:
рҹҺЁвңЁ **This Weekend at The ArtsCenter вҖ” Get Creative!**

Looking for something fun, hands-on, and inspiring to do this weekend? The ArtsCenter has **3 unique classes** coming upвҖ”perfect for trying something new or bringing a friend!

рҹҢҷ **Friday Night Vision Boarding with an Eye on the Summer**
рҹ“… Friday, April 10 | 6:30вҖ“9:30 PM
рҹ’І $72
Kick off your weekend with creativity and intention. Guided by a board-certified art therapist, this fun and inspiring workshop helps you visualize your goals and dream up your ideal summer.

рҹ”Ё **Make Your Own Tin Can Collage**
рҹ“… Saturday, April 11 | 1:00вҖ“5:00 PM
рҹ’І $91
Curious about working with metal? Create your own 8" x 10" collage using repurposed tinвҖ”no experience needed! A perfect mix of creativity and hands-on making.

рҹ“ё **iPhone Photo Safari at the Carrboro FarmersвҖҷ Market**
рҹ“… Saturday, April 11 | 9:00вҖ“11:30 AM
рҹ’І $72
Capture the colors of spring with your iPhone! Join an award-winning instructor for a guided photo walk and learn how to turn everyday snapshots into intentional images.

рҹҢҹ All classes are beginner-friendly (ages 15+) and designed to spark creativity.

рҹ‘ү Spots are limitedвҖ”grab a friend and sign up today!

events.title: рҹҺЁ ArtsCenter Open House
events.description: Mark your calendars ! Sunday April 19th 2-4pm . Carrboro is going to be the place to be ! Wander over to us here at the ArtsCenter and get a taste of all that we offer рҹҳҺ ! Fun for the whole family ! art, music, ceramics, good beer on draft, James Wallace on piano - what could be better ?!

events.title: рҹҺЁ Black Sheep Improv Show
events.description: We are going to be closed tomorrow morning 4/3 ! We have the Spring Fever ! Come to see us when we open up at 4pm and donвҖҷt forget about the Black Sheep Improv show at 7:30 !! It feels great to laugh рҹҳӮ. The Ceramics studio will operate as per usual. Happy Spring everyone ! рҹҗЈ

events.title: рҹӨқ WSDash Baseball Opening Game
events.description:
вҡҫпёҸ Play ball! Opening Weekend with the wsdashbaseball is finally here, and we couldnвҖҷt be more excited for another unforgettable season at the ballpark! рҹҺү

From the crack of the bat to the smell of ballpark favorites, thereвҖҷs something for everyone this year. Be sure to check the link in their bio for:
рҹҺҹпёҸ Single-game tickets now on sale
рҹҺ“ College student passes
рҹ‘§ The 2026 Kids Dash Club
рҹҡ— Parking prices and season passes
вҖҰand so much more!

рҹҚ” Come hungry! The Dash have revamped their menu items and offerings, and each of the three main concession windows features something different, so take a lap and explore them all before deciding on your game-day bite.

рҹҺҶ Stick around after the game for fireworks, and donвҖҷt forget that kids under 12 can run the bases once a waiver is signed, making it the perfect family outing.

HereвҖҷs to sunny days, hometown pride, and a season full of Dash baseball. вҡҫпёҸрҹ’ҷ

events.title: рҹҺө An Evening with the Galentines
events.description:
Happy to announce An Evening with thegalentines.band in  Gas Hill Drinking Room on July 8, 2026! Doors at 7 PM, show at 8 PM.

Tickets are on sale NOW at TheRamkat.com! 

The Galentines are an all-girl indie-rock band who are the very heart of the local music scene in Boulder, Colorado. They are known for their stunning harmonies blended into epic instrumental solos that remind the listener of classic rock bands such as Fleetwood Mac and Pink Floyd, while maintaining their punk edge with biting lyricism. Their music вҖңcelebrates the complexity of womanhood, capturing all of its challenges and triumphsвҖқ (Medium), while breaking the barriers that define the female experience. Encompassing multiple genres, including indie, punk and rock, the girls just released their heavily anticipated single Eden, which is now available on all streaming platforms. #thegalentines #gashilldrinkingroom #wsnc #supportlivemusic

events.title: рҹҺө Nether Hour
events.description:
Happy to welcome netherhour  in from Austin, Texas to Gas Hill Drinking Room on June 24, 2026! Doors at 7 PM, show at 8 PM.

Tickets are on sale NOW at TheRamkat.com!

вҖңWe donвҖҷt play country music, but weвҖҷre good ole boys from the country.вҖқ

Down in the heart of Austin, TX, where the music runs thicker than the heat, thereвҖҷs a band thatвҖҷs been making some serious noise. They call themselves Nether Hour, and theyвҖҷve been turning every dive bar and backroad juke joint into a full-blown musical revival. The band is a trio of unapologetic rebels, each one a powerhouse of sonic defiance. Bobby Flaco delivers gritty vocals and razor-sharp guitar riffs, while Bishop unleashes searing lead guitar and mandolin work, with Bercy laying down bass lines that slam right into your gut. Nether Hour is a relentless sonic melting pot, fusing the swampy grooves of Louisiana with Texas-sized doses of funk, blues, soul, and rock вҖҳnвҖҷ roll. #netherhour #gashilldrinkingroom #wsnc #supportlivemusic

events.title: рҹҺө Al Olender with Fruit Bats
events.description:
Excited  to welcome alolender to The Ramkat on July 16, 2026 in support of Fruit Bats! 

Get your tickets NOW at TheRamkat.com!

Songwriter Al Olender tries to grasp the brief, beautiful moments that make up our lives in slow-motion but finds that the tighter she holds on, the more these frames fall into a soupy blur. Change is our only constant, but what if we wanted to steep in the now? What if we could stay right here? Olender captures the anxieties of an ever-moving world with a sweet and silly sincerity on her second full-length album The Worrier, never shying away from staring the truth right in the face. Exploring anxiety, sexuality and anarchy, both in herself and in her relationships, OlenderвҖҷs acoustic, bluesy melodies point to the butterflies of first crushes, the gut-punching realities of responsibility and the eternal romance of friendship. #alolender #fruitbats #theramkat #wsnc #supportlivemusic

events.title: рҹҺө An Evening with Tyler Ramsey and Carl Broemel
events.description:
Very excited to announce An Evening with tylerramseymusic and carlbroemel (of mymorningjacket) at The Ramkat on August 7, 2026, in support of "Celestun," the duo's acclaimed new album! Doors at 7 PM, show at 8 PM.

Tickets go on sale at 10 AM Eastern this Friday, May 29, at TheRamkat.com! 

Having been friends and occasional touring partners for well over a decade, Asheville, NC-based singer-songwriter-guitarist Tyler Ramsey and My Morning Jacket guitarist Carl Broemel have at long last made their full-length recorded debut with Celestun out January 15. A marvel of interwoven musicianship and testament to the duoвҖҷs singular camaraderie, the album encapsulates the two veteran guitarist-songwritersвҖҷ mutual admiration and effortless compatibility as they swap and share nine new songs of dazzling dexterity and grace recorded almost entirely recorded on acoustic guitars. Though their initial objective had been all instrumental, the natural flow of the sessions led the duo to begin incorporating vocal tracks. Stark yet intricately arranged songs such as the cinematic title track, вҖңFlying ThingsвҖқ (featuring additional vocals from GRAMMYВ® Award-nominated singing siblings The Secret Sisters), and an incandescent version of Neil YoungвҖҷs вҖңSail AwayвҖқ are rich with dazzling dexterity and grace, RamseyвҖҷs nimble fingerstyle picking and BroemelвҖҷs classically trained virtuosity aligning to create a predominantly instrumental song cycle of rustic beauty and atmospheric power. Music at its most elemental, Celestun sees Carl Broemel and Tyler Ramsey stripping away artifice to unlock and explore deeply personal themes of wanderlust and familial love, of fellowship and communal adventure.#tylerramsey #carolbroemel #celestun #theramkat #wsnc #supportlivemusic

events.title: рҹҺЁ ADWS Annual Meeting
events.description:
ART CRUSH is evolving, and weвҖҷre doing it with intention.

This year, weвҖҷre focusing on fewer, more thoughtfully planned events to create stronger experiences for artists, attendees, and the businesses that make the Arts District of Winston-Salem thrive.

HereвҖҷs what 2026 looks like:
рҹҢҲ May вҖ“ Color the District
рҹҸҷпёҸJuly вҖ“ Winston-Salem Weekend
рҹҺү September вҖ“ Hispanic Heritage Month
рҹӣҚпёҸ November вҖ“ Arts District Celebration

Each event is designed to feel meaningful, vibrant, and well-supported, with clearer themes, deeper engagement, and more space to do things really well.

WhatвҖҷs new for artists:
WeвҖҷre introducing a $25 participation fee with 35 paid artist spots per event, plus 5 scholarship-funded spots for emerging artists. This helps sustain ART CRUSH while keeping access and opportunity at the center of what we do.

One more shift:
WeвҖҷre moving away from annual memberships. Artists can now participate event by event: simple, flexible, and clear. (Full details coming to our website soon!)

Want the full story?
Join us for the ADWS Annual Meeting at chadschai 
рҹ“… February 27 | вҸ° 6:00вҖ“7:30 PM

Come curious. Bring a friend whoвҖҷs new to ART CRUSH. Stay for the conversation.вҖӢвҖӢвҖӢвҖӢвҖӢвҖӢвҖӢвҖӢвҖӢвҖӢвҖӢвҖӢвҖӢвҖӢвҖӢвҖӢ

events.title: рҹҺЁ Acting Through Song Masterclass with Kerry Butler
events.description:
Fresh off their Junior Theatre Festival run, look what our rising sTArs have coming up next! ta.rising.stars 

вңЁ An unforgettable experience is coming to Winston-Salem! вңЁ

рҹҺӯ Tony Award-nominated Broadway star Kerry Butler is headlining an exclusive Acting Through Song Masterclass & Rising sTArs Broadway Cabaret hosted by Theatre Alliance Rising sTArs! рҹҢҹ

Kerry Butler has graced the Broadway stage originating roles in Beetlejuice, Mean Girls, Xanadu, Little Shop of Horrors, Catch Me If You Can, Hairspray, and so many more! рҹҢҹ From Broadway to TV, sheвҖҷs done it all! рҹ“ә

рҹҺӨ In this masterclass, eight performers will work one-on-one with Kerry and all attendees will receive invaluable tips on audition techniques, character development, and vocal performance. PLUS, there will be a Q&A! рҹҷҢ

рҹҺ¶ After the class, join Kerry and local performers for a one-night-only cabaret event featuring show-stopping performances and behind-the-scenes stories from the Broadway world. рҹҺӯвңЁ

Want to sing on stage with Kerry? рҹҺӨ Registration for the masterclass includes a ticket to the cabaret and the chance to perform LIVE! рҹҺ¶рҹ’Ҙ

рҹ“… Masterclass: March 8th, 2026 | 1-5 PM
рҹ“Қ Location: Juxtaposition Studios, Winston-Salem, NC
рҹҺҹпёҸ Registration Fee: $75 | Register at link in comments 

рҹҢҹ Cabaret: March 8th, 2026 | 8 PM
рҹ“Қ Location: Winston-Salem Theatre Alliance
рҹҺҹпёҸ Tickets: $25 | Get yours here:  https://theatrealliance.ws/box-office/

DonвҖҷt miss your chance to learn from one of BroadwayвҖҷs brightest stars! вңЁ

#KerryButler #BroadwayMasterclass #TheatreAlliance #RisingStars #WinstonSalemEvents

events.title: рҹҺЁ The Rocky Horror Show (9th Annual Production)
events.description:
Halloween Double Feature at Winston-Salem Theatre Alliance!

вҖңThe Rocky Horror ShowвҖқ-LIVE! (9th Annual Production)

October 24, 25, 26, 29, 30, and November 1 at 7:30 PMвҖ” Nightly costume contest for $50 Village Tavern gift card!

Participation Kits available at the door for purchase. (Please donвҖҷt bring personal props, for our actorsвҖҷ safety!)

Run Time: 1 hour, 40 mins.  No Intermission.

Also playing:

вҖңAlien: The Musical ParodyвҖқ
October 9, 10, and 11 at 7:30 PM and October 12 at 2 PM

Run Time: 90 mins.  No Intermission.

$25/$23 for вҖңRocky Horror ShowвҖқ
$25/$23 for вҖңAlien: The Musical ParodyвҖқ

Tickets: https://theatrealliance.ws/box-office/

Both productions Rated R for adult language, sexual content 

Plenty of free parking onsite. 

Signature drinks available for both productions!

Season Sponsors:
Lewis & Keller Attorneys
YES! Weekly

events.title: рҹҺЁ This Side of Crazy
events.location: The Beall Theatre at Theatre Alliance, 651 Pilot View St NW, Winston Salem, NC 27101
events.description:
DonвҖҷt be an outsider looking in! Enjoy a matinee with us this Saturday!

вҖңThis Side of Crazy,вҖқ by Del Shores, is next in Theatre AllianceвҖҷs Beall Theatre! When three feuding sisters are summoned home by their popular gospel-music composing mother to perform one final musical concert, old tensions flare, and the outcome is anything but blessed and holy! Grab a front-row seat to the latest by the southern writer who brought us вҖңSordid Lives,вҖқ вҖңSouthern Baptist Sissies,вҖқ and вҖңThe Trials and Tribulations of a Trailer Trash Housewife!вҖқ

September 20 and 27 at 12 PM and 3 PM

Tickets: $15

Get them here: https://theatrealliance.ws/box-office/

Rated R for language, sexual content, mature subject matter

LOCATION: The Beall Theatre at Theatre Alliance - 651 Pilot View St NW Winston Salem, NC 27101

Signature Drink: The Sandi Patti

Running Time: 2 hours, with one 15-minute Intermission

(same physical building as Ihrie Theatre, but the entrance is on Pilot Street- free parking in the upper lot adjacent to the building. (The Centenary lot beside our building.))

Pictured: Katy Carroll

Photos: Neil Jester - Fine Art Photography

events.title: рҹҺЁ Hairspray
events.location: Winston-Salem Theatre Alliance's Jamie Lawson Auditorium, 650 W Sixth Ave, Winston-Salem, NC
events.description:
Back to Baltimore tonight for вҖңHairspray!вҖқ TonightвҖҷs forecast for rain presents an ideal opportunity to stay dry and enjoy an evening with a multi-Tony-Award-winning musical!

вҖңHairsprayвҖқ bursts onto the Theatre Alliance stage in September!  If you arenвҖҷt familiar with this colorful confection of a show, you should be!  Follow 1960s teeny-bopper Tracy Turnblad and her friends and family as they navigate the fight for civil rights and everyoneвҖҷs right to dance, because вҖңYou CanвҖҷt Stop The Beat!вҖқ Arguably featuring one of the best, high-energy scores in several decades, вҖңHairsprayвҖқ is a show that celebrates the best in all of us!

Performing these dates at Winston-Salem Theatre AllianceвҖҷs Jamie Lawson Auditorium, 650 W Sixth Ave, Winston-Salem, NC:

September 12-13, 19-20 at 7:30 PM
September 14 and 21 at 2 PM
ADDED: September 26 and 27 at 7:30 PM

Free parking onsite!

Tickets: https://theatrealliance.ws/box-office/

Pricing: $25/$23

Rated PG-13 for innuendo, mild language 

Run time: 2 hours, 15 mins with one Intermission 

Signature Drink: EdnaвҖҷs Elixir

Season Sponsors: 
Lewis & Keller Attorneys
YES! Weekly

Photo: Neil Jester - Fine Art Photography

events.title: рҹҺЁ The Shark is Broken
events.location: The Beall Theatre at Theatre Alliance, 651 Pilot View St, Winston-Salem, NC
events.description:
Break a Leg to our Cast and Crew of вҖңThe Shark is Broken,вҖқ opening this afternoon in the Beall Theatre at 3 PM and also playing Sunday at 5:30 PM! (Missing from photo are Jeremy Engel, Alex Fredwall, and John Wilson).

The first summer blockbuster movie is being filmedвҖ”but no one working on the film would know it. Dive deep into the tumultuous, murky waters of the making of a major motion picture with testy, feuding costars, unpredictable weather, and a shark prop whose constant breakdowns are looking like an omen for the future of the movie. In this comedy co-written by Ian Shaw and Joseph Nixon, the short tempers of Jaws stars Robert Shaw (father of co-writer Ian), Richard Dreyfuss, and Roy Scheider take center stage as they bond, argue, drink, gamble, and pray for an end to the shoot, not knowing it will change their lives forever

Make a double-feature of It and catch вҖңThe Prince of Egypt,вҖқ also playing this weekend!

The Beall Theatre at Theatre Alliance (upper level, upper parking lot), 651 Pilot View St, Winston-Salem, NC

Saturday, Aug 16 at 3 PM
Sunday, Aug 17 at 5:30 PM
Saturday, Aug 23 at 3 PM and 5:30 PM

Photos: Neil Jester - Fine Art Photography

Tickets: https://theatrealliance.ws/box-office/dl

рҹҺҹпёҸ Grab your Early Bird Drink Tickets NOW: вң… 2 for $5 вң… 4 for $10 -> https://www.roarws.com/event-details/bull-city-cider-festival

Don't sleep on this one! It's gonna be a weekend to remember! See you at ROAR!

events.title: рҹҢҲ Alphabet Movie Night: K for Kids - Into the Spiderverse
events.description:
ABCDEFGHIJ вҖңKвҖқ for Kids this Alphabet Movie Night we will be gathering at the lGBTQ Center of Durham Warehouse on Hunt for a Kid & Family Film. We will be watching the Marvel animated feature вҖңInto the Spiderverse!вҖқ This Saturday the 30th of May at 6:30pm we will all be friendly neighborhood SpiderFans!

We will have seats and popcorn a plenty, but please bring a blanket or pillow if you are a floor person. Also Pajamas and Stuffies always welcome, so you can make the transition from the movie to the bed that much easier!

Hope to see yall soon!

*Note: this movie , and our subsequent feature, were selected by audience members whom attended the last feature! So please join us and help us select the films you'd like to see.

#family #lgbtq #queer #kidsmovie #familyfun #community #pajamaparty #popcorn

events.title: рҹҢҲ Get Shit Done Evenings
events.description:
Some of the biggest barriers to survivor safety are finance related. While we do our best to put cash directly into survivors hands, in tandem, we thought it would be awesome to create a body doubling/coworking space where we can help each other executive function. As well as offer relevant financial resources! ThatвҖҷs why we are launching вҖңGet Shit DoneвҖқ evenings. They will occur biweekly and be available in person or online (email advocate@lgbtqcenterofdurham.org for the virtual link). If you or someone you know is a money expert who wants to share their knowledge with queer/trans survivors, please reach out!

Image Descriptions/Alt Text:
Slide 1: Background is a wood grain table but in a lavender and dark purple hue. On top of the table, acting as the base of the image, is an open spiral notebook. The metal spirals are gold and the paper is white and lined. There is a faded coffee mug ring on the paper. In the top right corner of the image is a crumbled up piece of paper. On the right side of the image is a black pen. At the bottom right corner of the image is a folded set of marble colored glasses. On the spiral notebook in a black pen-like handwriting font that reads вҖңвҖңGet Shit Done, Every Other TuesdayвҖқ. Below that reads вҖң(bolded) 5:30-7p, 114 Hunt StвҖқ all highlighted in purple. Below that it reads, вҖңIn person & Virtual OptionsвҖң (underlined)вҖқ With purple and pink stars on the right of it. Below that reads вҖңA virtual space to do the $$ things weвҖҷve all been avoiding. Come work on taxes, budgeting, filling out that form... whatever you need!вҖқ At the bottom of the notebook page is the SQSN mushroom logo and Southern Queer Survivor Network in all purple bold+caps font.

 #survivors #lgbtq #durhamnc #economicjustice #queer #trans

events.title: рҹҢҲ Monthly POC Trans Support Group
events.description: If you're a person of color Trans or gender nonconforming community member, join us for our Monthly POC Trans Support Group to discuss your trans experience and normalized joy in your life. The support group meets the last Wednesday of each month from 5 to 7 PM.

events.title: рҹҢҲ Pride Block Party
events.description:
Its pride month and we're ready to be OUT on the block! Calling all ages of youth and families in our communities come celebrate Pride with us June 13th ! Activities and fun for kids 0-24 (and their families) including drag, face painting, arts and crafts, performances, and more!

#PrideMonth #lgbtq #youth #family #downtownDurham #durhamnc

events.title: рҹҢҲ LGBTQ+ Families Art Party and Community Conversation
events.description: LGBTQ+ families with kids!! Help build the future of the Southern Queer Families Network next week by joining us for an art party and community conversation. We want to learn from you and build with you! Dinner provided. Registration in bio (click the "sprout" link and then on this image). Email families@lgbtqcenterofdurham.org with any questions!

events.title: рҹҢҲ Dress Up Tea Party
events.location: LGBTQ Center of Durham, 114 Hunt St, Durham, NC
events.description:
Put Some Play in your Day!

Welcome in Wednesday with Stormie Daie, meetups for youth 5-12, and families held at the LGBTQ Center of Durham at 114 Hunt St and participating community spaces.  This is your chance to engage the children in the LGBTQ Durham community by sharing space and activities together. Welcome in Wednesday will prioritize fun through social gathering and activities emphasizing nature, science, sharing, education, reading, and hands on crafts.

This time will serve to create connections amongst youth and families and strengthen community ties with and amongst younger lgbtq folks and their families.

As well we will inform the children and families with the happenings of the LGBTQ Center through partnerships with likeminded groups and community partners.

For questions, concerns, registration or interest in sponsoring/supporting Welcome in Wednesday, contact Stormie Daie at sdaie@lgbtqcenterofdurham.org

4/15 -  Flower Pressing/Pet Rock
4/22 -  Terrarium
4/29 - Tea Party
5/6 - Kiteing
5/13 - Face Paint
5/20 - Dress Up Tea Party
5/22 - Show and Tell Talent Show

*activities are subject to change due to weather and space/supply needs

This is not child care, parents or guardians or care takers are expected to be present.

events.title: рҹҢҲ Alphabet Movie Nite: Space Jam
events.description:
Let's get reeling and rolling for our first вҖңAlphabet Movie NiteвҖқ here at the Queerhouse Theater. This first screening is вҖңKвҖқ for the Kids with all ages welcome! 

We will be watching the 90s multimedia sports comedy animated classic вҖңSpace Jam,вҖқ starring NCвҖҷs very own, plus all your favorite cartoon athletes - and hosted by Durham's own, Stormie Daie! 

Screening is at May 9 at 6:30pm with a runtime of 1:30min. Space is ample but seating is limited so please bring a blanket or lawn chair if possible. 

Also feel free to bring your best stuffie and wear your best pjs so you can crawl home and get in bed promptly after the finish of the film! 

Popcorn provided. Feel free to bring outside meals/snacks for you and yours.

Come early - parking is limited, primarily street parking.

events.title: рҹҢҲ Welcome in Wednesday: Kiteing
events.location: LGBTQ Center of Durham, 114 Hunt St
events.description:
Put Some Play in your Day!

Welcome in Wednesday with Stormie Daie, meetups for youth 5-12, and families held at the LGBTQ Center of Durham at 114 Hunt St and participating community spaces.  This is your chance to engage the children in the LGBTQ Durham community by sharing space and activities together. Welcome in Wednesday will prioritize fun through social gathering and activities emphasizing nature, science, sharing, education, reading, and hands on crafts.

This time will serve to create connections amongst youth and families and strengthen community ties with and amongst younger lgbtq folks and their families.

As well we will inform the children and families with the happenings of the LGBTQ Center through partnerships with likeminded groups and community partners.

For questions, concerns, registration or interest in sponsoring/supporting Welcome in Wednesday, contact Stormie Daie at sdaie@lgbtqcenterofdurham.org

4/15 -  Flower Pressing/Pet Rock
4/22 -  Terrarium
4/29 - Tea Party
5/6 - Kiteing
5/13 - Face Paint
5/20 - Dress Up Tea Party
5/22 - Show and Tell Talent Show

*activities are subject to change due to weather and space/supply needs

This is not child care, parents or guardians or care takers are expected to be present.

events.title: рҹҢҲ Black Trans Short Film Festival
events.description:
WeвҖҷre proud to partner with Comfrey Films for the Black Trans Short Film FestivalвҖ”an inspiring celebration of bold, culture-shifting films by Black trans, gender non-conforming, and intersex filmmakers.
Register for free to join us at the Black Trans Short Film Festival in Durham, NC April 30-May 2nd. comfreyfilms.org/festival 

CanвҖҷt make it to Durham this month? Get your ticket to BTSFF virtual! 
This yearвҖҷs theme, вҖңWe are free now,вҖқ centers storytelling that does not ask for permissionвҖ”stories that are shaping culture, building community, and imagining new possibilities for the future.
The Black Trans Short Film Festival features a dynamic lineup of film screenings, filmmaker conversations, and community events that celebrate emerging and established voices in Black trans media.
Join us in supporting these incredible artists and experiencing the power of their work.
рҹҺ¬ Learn more and get tickets: comfreyfilms.org/festival
We hope to see you there!

events.title: рҹҢҲ Comfrey Films Black Trans Short Film Festival
events.description:
TDOV RECAP

Let's Reminisce on our Trans Day of Visibility Weekend last month! This was a partnership between BLMP SONG TLC and The LGBTQ Center of Durham. The weekend started with a Trans Short Film Screening in partnership with Comfrey Films and concluded with our Annual TDOV Tea. 

Don't forget to check out the Comfrey Films Black Trans Short Film Festival this weekend! 

officialblmp ignitekindred comfreyfilms translawcenter

events.title: рҹӨқ Get Out of My Dreams
events.description:
So remember when Arcana turned TEN in December, and Erin threw the dance party of her dreams with four of her favorite DJs? Well this is a party that just wonвҖҷt stop- we did it again in March, and now weвҖҷre BACK for our June Pride edition with Fifi Hifi and VSPRTN. Get Out of My Dreams, get into my car, and letвҖҷs road trip through time together! A  four hour dance party replete with all the sapphic longing and yearning and fantasizing we can fit into one night. Big sapphic dance hits, divas, and whatever else Fifi and Ves are feeling for this very special Pride party. Robyn, Prince, Florence, Whitney, Kylie, Prince, Janelle Monae, FKA Twigs, Gaga, Sia, Charli, and beyond.

Last December and March this party was in full swing by 10pm, so do arrive early!

This show will sell out! Get your $10 tickets in advance- link in bio.

 21+ with proper ID

events.title: рҹӨқ Pride Salon: Ambient Performances
events.description:
THIS FRIDAY рҹҘө join us for an evening of ambient performances at this low-key, sexy salon. IT'S PRIDE вңЁ so come see these performers move with lots of exactly THATрҹҢҲ

рҹҺ¶lepayne3 Sweet 'N Lo will be singing two cabaret songs to soothe you with

вқӨпёҸвҖҚрҹ”Ҙ_kaiology will be bringing some sultry energy with drag (psssst you remember this epic winner from the queer talent show рҹҸҶ)

рҹҳүdizquefresa Whitney (La TUYA) will be setting the scene in between with some music for your ears only рҹ«¶рҹ’Ӣ

21+

events.title: рҹӨқ Craft Night: Beginner Hand Sewing Demo
events.description:
TOMORROW! рҹӘЎ

River Takada (rivtak) (she/they) will be walking around helping trouble shoot your mending projects, and words of encouragement and tops of how to practically mend your texts. 

At 7:15 theyвҖҷll be doing a beginner hand sewing demo! 

Needles, thread, random fabric scraps, and scissors will be provided.

Bring 1-2 textile (clothing, bag, patching) mending projects you would like to ask questions about and get started on! 

NO hemming and alteration projects please! 

BYO zippers, buttons, matching thread or fabric for patching, etc. 

ItвҖҷs still craft night, so other craft projects are welcome to come and mingle with the sewing! 

(Free event but tips for River are always welcome!)рҹ’ё

events.title: рҹӨқ Forever Barbara screening at Full Frame Documentary Film Festival
events.location: Full Frame Documentary Film Festival, Durham
events.description:
Arcana is proud sponsors of the Full Frame Documentary Film Festival happening this weekend in Durham!! 

There are some great documentaries happening this weekend, but weвҖҷre especially excited for FridayвҖҷs screening of Forever Barbara: 

Feminist icon and lesbian filmmaker Barbara HammerвҖҷs life mission was to create and record lesbian histories, both personal and societal. With HammerвҖҷs own voice as our guide, Barbara Forever weaves elements from her personal archiveвҖ”films, home movies, photos, audio recordingsвҖ”and interviews with her longtime partner, Florrie Burke, to create an expansive portrait of its subject. 

playing at 7:30! tickets at fullframefest !!! 

Stop in before or after the screening for a springtime cocktail! If you pop in after, you might catch Ally J and Kevin ClarkвҖҷs set!!

We also have live music this week with many many may long time friends: Emily & Itsi, Ally & Kevin, and Les Trios Chats!!! some folk, acoustic, and live jazz!!! so fun! 

come make new friends, come hang out!!

events.title: рҹҢҲ TRAMP Dance Party
events.description:
GAG! is launching a dance party on January 9th! TRAMP was created in response to the community for a dance party вҖ” a raw, no disruptions, nonstop music dance party where you can dance freely. Once Bitter Social opened, a new downtown bar by the makers of Bitter Sweet and Johnson Street Yacht Club, it became clear this space was made for just that!

This small intimate space creates a different vibe from any GAG party that promotes getting closer with each other; whether itвҖҷs dancing or conversing in one their dark corners.
GAG is bringing two special guest DJs, featuring, Pisces Prince (Gaven Simon) from NYC вҖ” featured in So.Gay in 2025 and GHO3 SPINZ from GAG to Death this past Halloween, will pump up the BPMs FOR EVERYONE tramping on the dance floor!

Not only is it a premiere of a new party вҖ” but it serves as a birthday celebration of GAG producer, Trey Roberts!

events.title: рҹҢҲ Camp ASPYRE 2026 Registration Opens
events.description:
Registration for Camp ASPYRE 2026 opens in exactly ONE WEEK on June 1st!! Heart + share this if you're as excited as we are!

ASPYRE Youth Leadership Camp is a powerful experience designed to empower young members of the LGBTQ community and their allies to achieve personal growth through advocacy, activism, and leadership development. Campers will build confidence, learn how to organize and advocate for their communities, and connect with other young people who are passionate about creating positive change.

ASPYRE Youth Leadership Camp welcomes youth ages 13 to 18 who are still in high school and are ready to step into leadership and make a meaningful difference in their communities.

Have questions? Reach out to the ASPYRE team at aspyre@lgbtcenterofraleigh.com for more information.

#CampASPYRE #ASPYRE2026
